text.title: Unit Testing — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when unit-testing is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.description: test_user_create with five asserts on different concerns.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: First failure masks the rest; name lies about what is tested. Fix: Split into one test per behaviour.

antipattern.description: Unit test calls httpx.get on a live URL.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Slow, flaky, depends on external service. Fix: Mock at the boundary.

antipattern.description: mock.assert_called_with very specific args, including incidental defaults.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Refactors break tests that should not change. Fix: Assert essential args only; use mocker.ANY for incidental.
Root cause: Name conveys nothing; CI failures unactionable. Fix: test_<behaviour>_when_<context>.
Root cause: Order-dependent failures. Fix: Use fixtures with teardown.
